title: Email Notification Email Text should not be put into Subject line

posts.author: meddle01 (John Gray)
posts.cookedHtml:
When you create a Notification automation, and it emails, it stuffs the contents of the entire email into the subject line.

Subject should be a field and separate from the body text.

Affect: Email notifications go out to clients looking horrible and unprofessional because of this. If you bring up this email in web email client, you literally have to scroll down through the subject to get to the email. It just looks bad.

Thank you!

posts.author: MarkP (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: Couldn’t agree more. Without this feature, email notifications from monday are more or less useless because of how unprofessional they appear in the recipient’s email inbox.

posts.author: jhanold (Julia Hanold | OrangeDot | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: I have also run into this issue with a client and would love to hear if a solution is in the works.

posts.author: Skyoso (Richard)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is also an issue I’m trying to fix too.
The subject needs to be a setting, not a giant list of information that ruins the functionality of notifications.

I’d like to be informed when this is addressed.
Please let us know if a solution for this is found or if the issue is fixed.

posts.author: Skyoso (Richard)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just found out that there is a different automation that can be selected. Either “gmail” or “outlook” instead of “notify” It seems like that adds the control to set the subject.

monday-mail-notification-screenshot1155×1107 21.6 KB

posts.author: LCenter (Lori)
posts.cookedHtml: The email feature unfortunately doesn’t support subitem information so the only option is the “notify” feature with a full subject line.

posts.author: Urszula (Urszula Janeczko)
posts.cookedHtml: This is so annoying and unprofessional looking. It should be possible to edit the Subject line

posts.author: meddle01 (John Gray)
posts.cookedHtml:
This seems like really low hanging fruit.

The way these alerts come across does look very unprofessional.

Thanks for voting!

posts.author: StacyB (Stacy Bowman)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree!

posts.author: jwebster (Jane W)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, this would be a vast improvement on the notification feature.

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @meddle01,
You can also try EasyMails app to design (drag and drop builder). you can choose the board columns in the subject and body too.

posts.author: amy.ko (Amy Ko)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree here. I don’t want to use the email feature since it has to be connected to a Gmail account. If I leave my job, the automation would fail. Updating the subject line in the notification feature is more sustainable for the program in the long run.

posts.author: MondayDrew (Drew Love)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update? I’m a bit shocked this hasn’t been addressed. You can do this in AirTable and it’s immensely useful.

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
@meddle01,

You can use EasyMails app to customise your subject and boy of the email.
It has drag & drop Email template designer, you can use your column as placeholder in your email template.

posts.author: Yash.Gangani (Yash Gangani)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @meddle01

Check out EasyMails where you can customize your mail template using drag and drop editor.
You can use that as a notification emails using personal branding.

posts.author: th3p3arl (Earl Dauley)
posts.cookedHtml: I am shocked to be reading posts from two years ago about this, and it still seems unresolved. Monday dev team, can we please prioritize this? A huge block of text as the subject line is unacceptable when using a tool that’s had this many price increases!

posts.author: cspahr (Cam Spahr)
posts.cookedHtml: IF this feature is really not possible, I would rather have a default subject line of Monday.com Automation than just having the entire body in the subject line. Ideally, we should be able to customize both, but if it’s really not possible, I don’t know why the default needs to be the entire body in the subject line.

posts.author: cspahr (Cam Spahr)
posts.cookedHtml: Problems like this is why I am amazed Monday.com has good reviews, this ERP is extremely limiting in the weirdest of ways.

posts.author: AmyCACI (Amy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree here - we are currently in a trial - deciding between Asana and Monday.com - I am very disappointed with the limited functionality of Monday - examples:
No subtasks of subtasks
No ability to customize notification subject
No ability to notify multiple email addresses in one automation unless subscribers

I am also not thrilled and the length of time it seems for Monday.com to work on feature upgrades requested by users.

Hoping there is a response on a timeline to this thread.

posts.author: ITBill (Bill Moller)
posts.cookedHtml:
From another thread…

Screenshot 2025-01-16 at 3.07.57 PM2048×1976 480 KB

Come on…

posts.author: ITBill (Bill Moller)
posts.cookedHtml: I would prefer not to grant an additional third-party access to ALL of my data…

posts.author: ITBill (Bill Moller)
posts.cookedHtml: My company doesn’t use free gmail or outlook accounts. It’s odd to me that this is the integration that Monday.com chose to add, given one assumes most Monday.com users are business users?

posts.author: ITBill (Bill Moller)
posts.cookedHtml: Really low hanging fruit… add a field to workflow/automation notifications… use that field for subjects instead of entire notification when sending mail…

posts.author: ITBill (Bill Moller)
posts.cookedHtml: Cannot agree more, +1, pages of notification data in a subject line is… less than ideal

posts.author: mary-mercado (Mary Mercado)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why is this still an issue years later? This seems like a simple fix that will yield better outcomes across all instances.

Monday DEV Team - this please help!

posts.author: rbeckwith (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! This would definitely be helpful as the notification feature is a lot more useful in certain cases than the actual outlook integration.

posts.author: BKFA (Kate)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote for this VITAL update, please!
